segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: OK, now are there any questions before we ...
segments.zh: 好了，在我们……之前，还有什么问题吗？

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: How are you getting on with your art project, Tom?
segments.zh: 汤姆，你的艺术项目进展如何？

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 还行吧。他们给了我们鸟类的主题作为项目基础，但我对野生动物其实没那么感兴趣。不过我开始慢慢投入了，引言部分基本完成了。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Like, they gave us the theme of birds to base our project on, and I'm not really all that interested in wildlife.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: But I'm starting to get into it.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: I've pretty well finished the introductory stage.

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: So have I.
segments.zh: 我也是。他们发那些列着书籍和网站的资料时，我真的很抵触，但读得越多，就越感兴趣。

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: When they gave us all those handouts with details of books and websites to look at, I was really put off, but the more I read, the more interested I got.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Me too.
segments.zh: 我也是。我发现可以研究鸟类在艺术中的很多不同方面——色彩、动态、质感。所以我很期待去鸟类公园参观。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: I found I could research so many different aspects of birds in art – colour, movement, texture.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: So I was looking forward to the Bird Park visit.

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: What a letdown!
segments.zh: 真让人失望！下起了倾盆大雨，我们几乎一只鸟都没看到。比去自然历史博物馆那次差远了。

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: It poured with rain and we hardly saw a single bird.

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: Much less use than the trip to the Natural History Museum.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是啊。我喜欢那里关于进化的所有内容。和弗莱彻博士的工作坊也很棒，尤其是头脑风暴环节。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: I liked all the stuff about evolution there.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: The workshop sessions with Dr Fletcher were good too, especially the brainstorming sessions.

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: I missed those because I was ill.
segments.zh: 我生病了，错过了那些。真希望我们能看看去年学生做的项目。

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: I wish we could've seen the projects last year's students did.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Mm.
segments.zh: 嗯，我觉得他们是想让我们做自己的东西，而不是抄袭。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: I suppose they want us to do our own thing, not copy.

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: Have you drafted your proposal yet?
segments.zh: 你的提案草稿写好了吗？

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Yes, but I haven't handed it in.
segments.zh: 写好了，但还没交。我需要修改一些部分。我意识到我的研究笔记几乎全是描述，实际上没有做任何评价。所以我得改一下。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: I need to amend some parts.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: I've realised the notes from my research are almost all just descriptions, I haven't actually evaluated anything.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: So I'll have to fix that.

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: Oh, I didn't know we had to do that.
segments.zh: 哦，我不知道我们还得做那个。我也得看看。你做了项目时间表吗？

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: I'll have to look at that too.

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: Did you do a timeline for the project?

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Yes, and a mind map.

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: Yeah, so did I.
segments.zh: 是啊，我也做了。我挺喜欢的。但是要解释我行动计划中决策的依据很难。

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: I quite enjoyed that.

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: But it was hard having to explain the basis for my decisions in my action plan.

items.prompt: Which TWO parts of the introductory stage to their art projects do Jess and Tom agree were useful?

pairs.note: useful 对应 use，注意词性变化

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是 introductory stage 中有用的部分，原文提到 'Much less use than the trip to the Natural History Museum'，说明 trip 是有用的，对应选项 C。

items.prompt: Which TWO parts of the introductory stage to their art projects do Jess and Tom agree were useful?

pairs.note: 题干说'useful'，原文说'got interested'，表示有用导致感兴趣，是同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位词'introductory stage'找到原文，然后注意'useful'被替换为'interested'，表示他们觉得有用。

items.prompt: In which TWO ways do both Jess and Tom decide to change their proposals?

pairs.note: change 替换为 amend，proposals 替换为 parts
change 替换为 fix，proposals 隐含在上下文中

paraphrase.explain: 题干问两人决定如何修改提案，定位句提到需要修改部分内容，其中 'amend some parts' 和 'fix that' 对应 'change'，且 'proposals' 由上下文推断。

items.prompt: In which TWO ways do both Jess and Tom decide to change their proposals?

pairs.note: change 替换为 amend，proposals 替换为 parts，意思相同

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位句中的'amend some parts'和'haven't evaluated anything'，可以推断出需要修改提案中的描述部分并加入评价，这与选项E（添加评价）一致。
